description_long: This is a three-story bank building with a symmetrical facade, featuring a prominent arched double-door entrance at the ground level. The exterior is finished in light beige and cream tones, with classical architectural details such as pilasters, cornices, and window frames. The uppermost section of the facade displays a large Euro currency symbol in gold, set within a decorative pediment. The windows are evenly spaced and rectangular, with the first and second floors having more ornate framing. The sides and rear of the building are plain, lacking windows or decorative elements. The overall design suggests a neoclassical influence, conveying stability and formality.
description: A neoclassical-style bank building with a grand arched entrance, gold Euro symbol signage, and ornate facade details in beige and cream.
description_view_0: Front view showing the arched double-door entrance, six windows, and a gold Euro symbol atop the facade.
description_view_1: Left side view revealing a plain, windowless wall and the building's layered roof structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a completely flat, undecorated wall with no windows or doors.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, with a plain wall and visible roof overhang.
attribute_signage: A large gold Euro currency symbol is centered at the top of the front facade within a decorative pediment.
attribute_entrance_design: Main entrance features a double arched doorway, likely made of wood or metal, framed by classical columns and decorative molding.
